macro_segments.title: Introduction to the Mishnah: Entitlement to the Skin Based on Altar's Merit in the Flesh

micro_segments.title: Opening: The shift from meat to skin and the principle of zachah
Rashi's explanation: Psul before zerikah invalidates both meat and skin
Discussion of tame and neveilah: When invalidity occurs and its effect on skin rights
The concept of 'olat ish': That the korban must have counted for someone at the time of blood-throwing

macro_segments.title: Extension of Skin Rights to All Korban Types and the Role of the Altar's Merit

micro_segments.title: Mishnah rule: Echo olat ha'ish, venechasim kalim versus kodshei kodashim distribution
Citation of Leviticus 7:8 concerning the kohen's entitlement to the skin of the olah
Kal vachomer argument: From olah lacking flesh to kodshei kodashim having flesh
Mizbeiach yochiach: Countering the objection that the altar doesn't receive skin

macro_segments.title: Clarification of 'Olat Ish' Exclusions and the Principle of Personal Versus Communal Offerings

micro_segments.title: Tanu Rabbanan: Excluding olat hekdesh (communal offerings) from the rule via Rabbi Yehuda
Clarification: Motarot (surplus funds) from asham and chatat that become olah
Rabbi Abba and Rabbi Yannai: Case of sanctifying an animal both as olah and to bedek habayis
Gemara analysis: Whether the skin becomes owned by bedek habayis even after redemption

macro_segments.title: Case of One Who Sanctifies All Property: Determining Meat versus Skin Designation

micro_segments.title: Mishnah: Hamakdish nechasav—interpreting sanctification of all property including animals
Rabbi Eliezer's position: Unblemished animals sold to those bringing sacrifices
Rabbi Yehoshua's position: Contextual interpretation—males as olah, females' proceeds for olah
Gemara clarification: Even for Rabbi Yehoshua, the skin remains bedek habayis property

macro_segments.title: Filtering the Word 'Ish' and Establishing That Merit, Not Person, Determines Kohanim's Entitlement

micro_segments.title: Tanu Rabbanan: How 'ish' is understood to include women, slaves, and converts via textual balance
Olah she'alah le-ish: That the offering must have counted, excluding pre-zerikah psul cases
Challenge: Whether shelo lishmah (not for its name) also excludes kohanim from skin
Or ha'olah: Demonstrating that any valid korban, regardless of attribution, grants skin rights

macro_segments.title: Extension to All Kodashim and Rabbi Yishmael's Assumption of Natural Unity Between Flesh and Skin

micro_segments.title: Tanu Rabbanan: How to derive skin rights for kodshei kodashim using asher hikriv
Rabbi Yishmael's view: Kal vachomer approach and assumption that skin naturally follows meat
Parim hanisrafim and chatat/asham: Examples where skin inherently goes with flesh
Rabbi's position: Only by olah does Torah need to specify skin allocation

macro_segments.title: Qualification: Only Kohanim Fit to Perform the Service Receive Skin Rights

micro_segments.title: Exclusion of tuvul yom, mechusar kipurim, and onen based on hakohen hamakriv
Distinction between meat (for consumption) and skin (not for consumption) fitness
Lo yihyeh linkage to asham: Deriving that bones, like skin, belong to the kohen

macro_segments.title: Critical Moment for Skin Entitlement: Before or After Flaying, and at Time of Blood-Throwing

micro_segments.title: Mishnah: Kodashim with psul before flaying versus after—determining when kohanim lose skin rights
Rabbi Chanina Sgan Hakohanim: Never saw flayed skin burned—skin always goes to kohanim once separated
Chachamim response: Absence of evidence is not proof; burning separate flayed skin is possible
Contradiction between earlier mishnahs: Time of blood-throwing versus time of flaying as critical moment

macro_segments.title: Reconciling Mishnahs: Flaying versus Blood-Throwing as the Determining Factor—Two Interpretive Approaches

micro_segments.title: Abaye's approach: Both mishnahs follow Rabbi; flaying is theoretically decisive, but zerikah is practical reality
Rav's approach: Both mishnahs follow Rabbi Eliezer; kodem/achar hefshet means before/after zerikah readiness

topical_tags.term: Rabbi Eliezer vs. Rabbi Yehoshua (on property sanctification)
tuvul yom (one who immersed on that day)
Rabbi Yishmael (kal vachomer and natural unity of flesh/skin)
shelo lishmah (not for its [the offering's] name)
